content: 'Minor' means a person who, under the Majority Act 1875, is deemed not to have attained majority. 'Guardian' means a person having the care of the person of a minor, or of the minor's property, or of both. 'Ward' means a minor for whose person or property, or both, there is a guardian.

section: Section 7 - Power of the Court to make order as to guardianship
content: Where the Court is satisfied that it is for the welfare of a minor that an order should be made appointing a guardian of the minor's person or property (or both), or declaring a person to be such a guardian, the Court may make an order accordingly. Such an order implies the removal of any guardian not appointed by will, other instrument, or by the Court. Where a guardian has already been appointed by will, instrument, or the Court, a new appointment cannot be made until the existing guardian's powers have ceased under this Act.

section: Section 8 - Persons entitled to apply for a guardianship order
content: An application for a guardianship order may be made by: the person desiring to be, or claiming to be, the guardian of the minor; any relative or friend of the minor; the Collector of the district where the minor resides or has property; or the Collector having authority over the class to which the minor belongs.

An application regarding guardianship of the person of a minor must be made to the District Court having jurisdiction where the minor ordinarily resides. An application regarding guardianship of property may be made to the District Court where the minor resides or where the property is located.

section: Section 17 - Matters to be considered by the Court in appointing a guardian
content: In appointing or declaring a guardian, the Court is guided by what appears, consistent with the law to which the minor is subject, to be for the welfare of the minor. The Court considers the age, sex and religion of the minor, the character and capacity of the proposed guardian, the proposed guardian's nearness of kin to the minor, the wishes of a deceased parent if any, and any existing or previous relationship between the proposed guardian and the minor or the minor's property. If the minor is old enough to form an intelligent preference, the Court may consider that preference. The Court will not appoint or declare a guardian against that person's will.

section: Section 19 - Guardian not to be appointed by the Court in certain cases
content: The Court cannot appoint or declare a guardian of the property of a minor whose property is under the superintendence of a Court of Wards. The Court also cannot appoint a guardian of the person of a minor who is a married female whose husband is not, in the Court's opinion, unfit to be her guardian, or of a minor whose father is living and is not, in the Court's opinion, unfit to be guardian of the minor's person.

section: Section 24 - Duties of guardian of the person
content: A guardian of the person of a ward is charged with the ward's custody, and must look after the ward's support, health, education, and other matters required by the law to which the ward is subject.

section: Section 25 - Title of guardian to custody of ward
content: If a ward leaves or is removed from the custody of a guardian of the person, the Court may, if it considers it for the welfare of the ward, order the ward's return to the guardian's custody, including by causing the ward to be arrested and delivered to the guardian. If the ward is a female who should not be compelled to appear in public, her production must accord with the customs and manners of the country. The ward residing against the guardian's will with a person who is not the guardian does not by itself terminate the guardianship.

The Court may remove a guardian (whether appointed or declared by the Court, or appointed by will or other instrument) for reasons including: abuse of trust; continued failure to perform duties; incapacity; ill-treatment or neglect of the ward; disregard of the Act or a Court order; conviction of an offence implying a character defect unfitting the person to be guardian; having an interest adverse to faithful performance of duties; ceasing to reside within the Court's jurisdiction; bankruptcy or insolvency (for a property guardian); or the guardianship ceasing under the law to which the minor is subject.

section: Section 41 - Cessation of authority of guardian
content: The powers of a guardian of the person cease upon: the guardian's death, removal or discharge; the Court of Wards assuming superintendence of the ward's person; the ward ceasing to be a minor; in the case of a female ward, her marriage to a husband who is not unfit to be her guardian; or, where the ward's father was unfit to be guardian, the father ceasing to be unfit.

An appeal lies to the High Court from orders made under this Act including: appointing, declaring, or refusing to appoint a guardian (Section 7); returning an application (Section 9); making or refusing an order for return of a ward to the guardian's custody (Section 25); refusing leave for removal of a ward from the Court's jurisdiction (Section 26); removing a guardian (Section 39); refusing to discharge a guardian (Section 40); and orders imposing penalties (Sections 44-45).
